hypothesis: A physics-informed Δ-learning model that augments the GFN1-xTB–DFT correction with transition-metal–specific descriptors (e.g., d-electron count, oxidation state, and a Hubbard U proxy) will yield lower prediction error for HSE06 band gaps in MOFs containing 3d transition metals (Cr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni) than Δ-learning using only structural descriptors, as demonstrated by a statistically significant reduction in MAE on a hold-out set enriched with such metals.
